response: The descendants of the group of black Indians associated with the Seminole people settled in El Nacimiento, Coahuila, Mexico, and in areas of Florida and Oklahoma. They were descendants of Black Seminoles who escaped slavery in the United States and formed a group centered in El Nacimiento, Múzquiz Municipality, Coahuila. These individuals also settled in Florida and Oklahoma, where they formed bands and were part of the Seminole people.
